based_on: Byte-precise mutation of fixtures/external/pytorch.adam.multi-step.golden.jsonl. v0.9.1 Adam multi-step adversarial plate.

mutation.explanation: Adam timestep MUST advance by exactly 1 per training step (Kingma & Ba 2014 Alg 1: t <- t + 1 once per step). Mutating step 1's t from 2 to 3 skips a step. Rule 25 catches the monotonicity violation (expected receipts[1].t == receipts[0].t + 1 = 2; got 3).

reconciliation_check_targeted_first: Rule 23: Adam bias correction t consistency — receipts[1].optimizer_config.t=3 but step_index=1 (expected t === step_index + 1 = 2). Rule 23 fires PER-RECEIPT before Rule 25's multi-step cross-receipt monotonicity check; both rules cross-fire on this fixture and either catches the off-by-one. Primary listed as Rule 23 to give Rule 23 dedicated bad-fixture coverage; Rule 25 has its own primary fixture (adam.bad-stale-moment-state).
purpose: v0.9.1 Adam multi-step anti-circularity fixture (kind: 'timestep-off-by-one'). Pressure-tests that the named multi-step rule fires BEFORE the reconciler consults fixture_status metadata.
v0_9_1_trust_framing: Adam multi-step rules 25 (state chain) + 26 (config constancy) are STRUCTURAL consistency checks. Producer-authenticity caveat per Rule 17 / Fang et al. 2023 applies.
